last_result: done — `go test ./...` flaked ~10% (3/30) on the auth tampered-key test. Root cause: it tampered an Argon2id hash by flipping the LAST base64 char of the 32-byte key, but that char carries 2 padding bits, so the flip frequently decoded to the same key (untampered → VerifyPassword correctly returns true → assertion fails). Fixed by tampering a real key byte (decode parts[5], key[0]^=0xFF, re-encode) → deterministic, 0/50 after. Pure test change, no production code. Reviewed (no blocking issues). Discovered while running the gate for the CREATE-via-PUBLIC item; that band-1 item was reverted untouched and remains open.
notes: Band-1 CREATE-via-PUBLIC residual still OPEN (next): on PG<=14 the role inherits CREATE on public via the PUBLIC pseudo-role; a per-role REVOKE can't strip it — must REVOKE CREATE ON SCHEMA public FROM PUBLIC and re-GRANT to indiepg_admin, scoped to the panel-managed `postgres` DB only (never operator app DBs — would break their apps). provisionSQL runs against `postgres`; the integration test connects there, so assert CREATE denial (42501) + admin-create-still-works there. Remaining band-1: that fix; secrets-at-rest (DB file 0600 + log-scrubbing test); login brute-force lockout (verify throttle is end-to-end tested via the login handler). Integration tests need a real PG and don't run in the normal gate; go/psql are snap-sandboxed (run with sandbox disabled).
north_star: An indie hacker can stand up and run a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI, on best defaults, with safe optional overrides.
